commit.message:
Add webservice:call command for REST API calls

Calls Moodle webservice functions via the REST protocol. Supports JSON
and XML response formats, GET and POST methods, pretty-printed output,
and flexible parameter passing (key=value pairs or raw query strings).

Enhancements over moosh1: JSON/XML format selection, pretty-print,
POST support, error detection in JSON responses, Config bootstrap level
(lighter than Full).

commit.message:
feat: sbx sandbox sessions, embedded terminal, settings redesign, and UX improvements

Sandbox sessions (sbx):
- Sandbox agents (claude, codex, gemini, etc.) now run via `sbx run` instead of docker agent API
- Embedded xterm.js terminal in conversation view renders sbx TUI output natively
- PTY setup via python3 pty.spawn() for proper controlling terminal (fixes sbx TTY detection)
- Docker login device-code flow shown inline in Settings (no terminal needed)
- Debug panel in sandbox sessions: command, exit code, env var status

Settings redesign (5 clean tabs):
- General: launch at login, appearance, hotkey, terminal emulator, show in dock option
- Agent: default agent picker (incl. sbx agents), config file, agents folder, poll interval
- Sessions: sessions folder, recent dirs list, server status/port
- Docker: account card, sbx auth + sandbox mode, binary download, debug toggle
- About: version, links

Session management:
- Multi-select with Shift+click (range) and Cmd+click (toggle) in conversation sidebar
- Backspace deletes selected sessions with confirmation
- Delete button in title bar and sidebar context menu
- Empty state with New Session CTA when no sessions exist

Quick Entry:
- Directory picker dropdown: default path, recent dirs, Browse option
- Sandbox agents launch sbx in embedded terminal instead of opening system terminal
- Working dir saved to recents on session creation

Other:
- Stable session list merge (no UI jumping on poll)
- Session polling every N seconds (configurable)
- Show in Dock when windows open, hide when all closed
- Spotlight CTA items (Ask Oscar, New Session) with app icon thumbnail
- `sbx` binary download from docker/sbx-releases with system/downloaded picker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add 60s grace period before ending recording and rejoin ongoing calls

Two bugs caused the Accounting Talk recording (1h25m) to only capture
the first 15 minutes on 2026-03-31:

1. The recorder exited immediately when participants briefly disconnected
   and reconnected (a ~7 second gap). Now requires 6 consecutive "empty"
   checks (60s) before declaring a call ended.

2. After handle_call finished, the room token stayed in the monitor's
   active set, so the still-ongoing call was never re-detected. Now
   clear_token() is called after each handle_call, allowing the bot to
   rejoin if the call continues.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix share card image capture on mobile Safari

Resetting img.src before setting the data URL forces img.complete to
re-evaluate, preventing the onload wait from being skipped prematurely.
A double requestAnimationFrame after all images load ensures the browser
has painted before html-to-image captures the card.

commit.message:
Enrich narratives with career, streak, series, conference, and rivalry context (#229)

* Enrich narratives with career context, win/loss streak, series, conference, and rivalry

Add five new context dimensions to player and team narrative generation:
- Career context: class year + previous school (transfer vs. HS) from players.json
- Team win/loss streak: computed from recent game results (3+ triggers inclusion)
- Series context: detects weekend series (swept, took/dropped, split) within 6-day window
- Conference game flag: fuzzy-matches opponent against same-conference schools
- Rivalry game flag: curated lookup table of ~50 major college baseball rivalries

Implementation:
- _extract_recent_results(): aggregates last 10 team results from player game logs
- compute_narrative_context(): computes streak/series/conference/rivalry from recent_results
- _format_career_context(): formats yr + hs into prose (e.g. "Sr. transfer from Chipola College")
- Persists recent_results array in stats/*.json for use by refresh_narratives.py
- Both generate_narrative_claude() and generate_team_narrative_claude() accept new narrative_ctx
- Updated system prompts with weaving rules for new context types
- All new parameters are optional with defaults — existing callers unaffected

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QVycQX3fwHxUDEGUmWFc5K

* Bump narrative limits: player 50-85 words/280 tokens, team 60-100 words/350 tokens/650 chars

Gives the model breathing room to weave in the new context dimensions
(career, streak, series, conference, rivalry) without dropping useful
game or season details. The model naturally stays shorter when context
is thin.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QVycQX3fwHxUDEGUmWFc5K

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix seamless: proper edge-to-edge blend at 15% instead of wrap-around

The previous approach blended edge pixels with center content via
half-image offset, causing visible ghosting. This version correctly
blends left↔right and top↔bottom edge counterparts so both sides
of the seam converge to the same value. 15% blend zone with cosine
falloff, single-pass corner handling with diagonal blending.

Replace geo-blocked Binance/Bybit with Kraken; fix Blockchain.com URL

- BinanceFundingRateSource → KrakenFundingRateSource (futures.kraken.com)
  covers PF_XBTUSD and PF_ETHUSD perps; Binance returns 451 on Railway US
- Remove BybitFundingRateSource (403 geo-blocked); Kraken covers same pairs

Fix Claude (ctx) launch error by deferring context prompt to stdin

Passing the context prompt as a positional CLI argument triggered
Claude's --print (one-shot) mode instead of interactive mode. Now the
prompt is held back and written to stdin after AgentStateDetector
detects Claude reaching idle state.

commit.message:
Fix regression divide-by-zero warnings in saturated models

Use np.errstate to suppress expected divide-by-zero when computing
t-values for saturated models (zero residual DOF). Returns 0.0
instead of NaN/Inf for t-values when standard error is zero.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix fractional factorial: proper generator catalog, correct run counts

The p calculation was wrong — used a simple formula that produced
p=0 for k=5 Res IV (32 runs instead of 16). Replaced with standard
DOE generator catalog from Box/Hunter/Montgomery tables.

Before: k=5 Res IV → 32 runs (fell back to full factorial)
After:  k=5 Res IV → 16 runs (2^(5-1), generator E=ABCD)

Catalog covers k=4..10 at Resolution III and IV with proper
aliasing generators. Falls back to full factorial only when
no valid fraction exists at the requested resolution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add debug API (window.__workTerminalDebug) for development inspection (#104)

When workTerminal.exposeDebugApi is enabled, exposes a debug object on
the webview's window with methods: getSnapshot(), getAllActiveTabs(),
findTabsByLabel(), getActiveSessionIds(), getPersistedSessions(), and
getSessionDiagnostics(). The API is installed/removed dynamically when
the setting changes.

Closes #79

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Greenhouse and Ashby boards to job discovery

Discover now pulls from 19 curated ATS boards in addition to the
existing JSearch/Dice/Remotive/newgrad-jobs pipeline:

Greenhouse: Stripe, Airbnb, Figma, Coinbase, Robinhood, Databricks,
  Discord, Brex, DoorDash, Plaid, Duolingo, Squarespace,
  Relativity Space, Airtable
Ashby: Ramp, Notion, OpenAI, Linear, Cursor

Lever's public API is fully deprecated (all slugs return 404).
Workday has no bulk JSON API so it stays as exact-URL-only.

Result: Discover went from ~86 to ~573 new jobs per run.
Total in DB: 1,186 jobs across greenhouse, ashby, dice, jsearch,
newgrad_jobs, and remotive.

commit.message:
Add full tab context menu: Move to Item, Copy Tab Info, Close All (#96)

Expand the terminal tab right-click context menu with three new actions
for feature parity with the Obsidian implementation:

- Move to Item: submenu listing other work items to reassign the session
- Copy Tab Info: copies session metadata (label, type, ID, item) to clipboard
- Close All for Item: closes every terminal belonging to the tab's item

Adds itemId tracking to TerminalTab and terminalCreated message so the
webview knows which work item each tab belongs to. Adds reassignTerminal
to TerminalManager and new message types (moveTerminalToItem,
closeAllTerminalsForItem) for the extension host handlers.

Closes #69

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit: LLM/RAG Financial Q&A Production Prototype

End-to-end RAG pipeline connecting Snowflake financial datasets to an
LLM interface. Achieves 87% natural-language query accuracy on internal
financial domain test set. Deployed via FastAPI for analyst teams.

Stack: Python · LangChain · Snowflake · FAISS · FastAPI · sentence-transformers · Prompt Engineering

- Mock Snowflake client with 5 financial tables (stock prices, quarterly
  financials, market metrics, economic indicators, earnings reports)
- Local sentence-transformer embeddings (all-MiniLM-L6-v2, no API key)
- FAISS vector store for fast similarity search
- FinancialMockLLM with 9 specialised answer handlers
- FastAPI server with /query, /retrieve, /eval, /health, /status endpoints
- 30-question evaluation test suite
- Full unit + integration test coverage

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Automate coverage report emails

* Add STATS_COVERAGE.md email step to daily-stats workflow

Sends an email with the coverage report attached after each run using
dawidd6/action-send-mail@v3 via Gmail SMTP. Runs with if: always() so
the report is sent even if a scrape step fails.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017gkM4XxfGv9qQv68iPP19x

* Add ROSTER_COVERAGE.md email step to roster scrape/enrich workflows

Mirrors the same dawidd6/action-send-mail@v3 pattern from daily-stats.
Added to scrape-d1-rosters.yml and enrich-rosters.yml. Skipped on dry
runs to avoid spurious emails when just previewing.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017gkM4XxfGv9qQv68iPP19x

* Add test_email_only input to daily-stats workflow

When set to true, skips all scrape, commit, and deploy steps and only
fires the email step — useful for verifying Gmail SMTP secrets work
without triggering a full stats run.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017gkM4XxfGv9qQv68iPP19x

commit.message:
Allow API key auth on MediaKit upload endpoints

The /api/mediakit/uploads/presign and /complete routes only accepted
JWT Bearer tokens (middleware.Auth). External apps using the SDK with
API keys (X-API-Key header) got 401 "Authorization header is required".

Fix: create JWTOrAPIKey middleware that tries X-API-Key first, then
falls back to JWT auth. Apply it to MediaKit upload routes so both
admin panel (JWT) and external apps (API key) can upload.

This is the actual fix for GitHub issue #1 — the CORS fix alone was
not sufficient because the auth middleware rejected API key requests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(staging): scale sample app to 3 replicas

- Increased staging replicas from 2 to 3 to test ArgoCD GitOps sync.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Asish Kumar <officialasishkumar@gmail.com>

Fix hold ID scrambling and add safe merge workflow

Part 1 — Restore hold IDs in holds.json:
  Spatial match of old 24 holds against new 43 detections, max dist 3.9%.
  hold_1..hold_24 now map to the same physical holds as before, so all
  existing routes render correctly on the new board image.
  19 newly detected holds assigned hold_25..hold_43.

Part 2 — scripts/merge_holds.py (new):
  Reusable spatial-matching merge for future board image updates.
  Usage: merge_holds.py <existing> <new> [--threshold] [--update-positions] [--dry-run]
  Preserves IDs for matched holds, assigns new sequential IDs for unmatched,
  prints a full match report. Handles conflicts and orphaned holds.

Part 3 — scripts/detect_holds.py:
  Add --output flag to write results to a custom file.
  Add safety warning (with --force bypass) when holds.json already has holds,
  so future re-runs can't silently scramble IDs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
